name: Yagi-Uda Array
description: A directional antenna array consisting of a driven element (typically a dipole) with one or more parasitic elements including reflectors and directors. The parasitic elements are not directly connected to the transmission line but are electromagnetically coupled to the driven element, creating a highly directional radiation pattern with significant forward gain.

driven_length.name: Driven Element Length
driven_length.description: Length of the driven dipole element, typically close to half wavelength

reflector_length.name: Reflector Length
reflector_length.description: Length of the reflector element, typically 5% longer than driven element

director_length.name: Director Length
director_length.description: Length of director elements, typically 5% shorter than driven element

reflector_spacing.name: Reflector Spacing
reflector_spacing.description: Distance between driven element and reflector

director_spacing.name: Director Spacing
director_spacing.description: Distance between driven element and first director, or between directors

num_directors.name: Number of Directors
num_directors.description: Number of director elements in the array

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves determining element lengths and spacings to achieve desired gain and impedance matching through electromagnetic coupling optimization.

steps.title: Determine Operating Frequency
steps.description: Calculate free-space wavelength and establish design frequency

steps.title: Design Driven Element
steps.description: Set driven element length for resonance, accounting for mutual coupling effects

steps.title: Position and Size Reflector
steps.description: Place reflector behind driven element with appropriate length for maximum reflection

steps.title: Design Director Elements
steps.description: Size directors shorter than driven element for forward wave enhancement

steps.title: Optimize Director Spacing
steps.description: Space directors for constructive interference in forward direction

steps.title: Calculate Input Impedance
steps.description: Determine impedance including mutual coupling effects between elements

steps.title: Optimize for Gain and SWR
steps.description: Fine-tune element lengths and spacings for maximum gain and acceptable SWR

equations.resonantFrequency: f0 = c / (2 * Ld_eff) where Ld_eff accounts for end effects and coupling
equations.inputImpedance: Zin = R11 + j*X11 + sum(Z1n * In/I1) for n parasitic elements
equations.gain: G = 4*pi*U_max/P_total where U_max is maximum radiation intensity

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Method of moments with mutual impedance matrix Z_mn = R_mn + j*X_mn between elements
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Superposition of individual element patterns with proper phase relationships from mutual coupling
keyAssumptions: Thin wire approximation valid for length/diameter > 100
Elements parallel and in same plane
Sinusoidal current distribution on each element

references: Balanis, C.A., 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 10
Stutzman, W.L. and Thiele, G.A., 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 7
Volakis, J.L., 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 10
